_comment: Curated Canadian census-division (CDUID) water-supply stress: chronic groundwater/capacity risk. severity 1-3 (1=elevated, 2=high, 3=severe, e.g. active development/water-servicing freeze). Wired into m_infrastructure (CA) water_security + the infrastructure.drought filter, mirroring the US county_groundwater hotspots. CDs not listed are treated as unstressed. Add entries as new regions are confirmed; cite a source.

3530.note: Groundwater/capacity crisis: ~80% of supply is groundwater; municipal system ran near 97% capacity for 3 consecutive years (safe target 80%); deep Waterloo Moraine aquifers (e.g. AFB2) down by metres; localized dry private wells and shrinking wetlands; temporary freeze on new water-servicing agreements and development approvals across parts of Kitchener/Waterloo and townships. Compounded by rapid growth: Kitchener-Cambridge-Waterloo CMA grew ~4.9% in 2024, Kitchener ~6% (2nd-fastest-growing city in Canada).
3530.source: Region of Waterloo groundwater interference policy update; EngageWR Water Supply Strategy; StatCan 2024 population estimates

5929.note: Recurring drought since 2022; local state of emergency declared when supply hit historic lows. Small coastal supply with limited storage and repeated summer shortfalls.

4811.note: Glacier-fed North Saskatchewan River supply; research flags heightened late-summer instability from glacier retreat for the >1 million Albertans reliant on the Bighorn Dam. Structural/forward-looking supply risk.
4811.source: The Weather Network (glacier retreat / Bighorn Dam supply risk)

4806.note: Glacier-fed Bow River supply; southern-Alberta drought cycles and glacier retreat reduce late-summer river stability underpinning municipal supply, hydropower and irrigation.
4806.source: The Weather Network; southern AB drought reporting

4802.note: Southern Alberta drought cycle; Oldman/St. Mary irrigation-and-municipal system under repeated water-shortage advisories; glacier-fed late-summer flows declining.

4801.note: Driest corner of Alberta; recurring drought and low river flows on the South Saskatchewan system.

4706.note: Southern Saskatchewan drought cycling; reliance on Buffalo Pound / Qu'Appelle system sensitive to prolonged dry periods.
